---
title: "Forest Canopy Cover and Lyme Disease Incidence in Endemic U.S. Counties"
author: "Juliana Carrillo"
date: "April 14, 2026"
format:
  pdf:
    mainfont: "Latin Modern Roman"
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{titling}
        \setlength{\droptitle}{-3em}
        \pretitle{\begin{center}\large\bfseries}
        \posttitle{\end{center}\vspace{-1em}}
        \preauthor{\begin{center}\small}
        \postauthor{\end{center}\vspace{-1.5em}}
        \predate{\begin{center}\small}
        \postdate{\end{center}\vspace{-1em}}
---


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(GGally)
library(sandwich)
library(stargazer)
library(janitor)
library(dplyr)
library(here)
library(car)
library(lmtest)
library(gridExtra)
library(moments)
library(psych)
library(sf)
library(tigris)
library(nhdplusTools)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(patchwork)
library(here)

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)
```

```{r set plotting theme}
theme_set(theme_minimal())

blue   <- '#2166AC'
orange <- '#D4760A'
```

## Data Loading and Cleaning

```{r, lyme data loading and cleaning}

lyme_raw    <- read.csv(here("data", "external", "lyme_prevalence", 
                              "LD_Case_Counts_by_County_2023_updated.csv"))

dim(lyme_raw)       
names(lyme_raw)      # Note to self, I found inconsistent capitalization Cases2018 vs cases2021 

```

```{r, inspecting response}

# checking the endemic status categories 

# checking if I have enough high incidence cases
table(lyme_raw$ststatus)

# checking FIPS formatting
range(lyme_raw$stcode)
range(lyme_raw$ctycode)

# reducing to only the columns of interest
lyme_r <- lyme_raw[, c("stname", "Ctyname", "stcode", "ctycode", "ststatus", "Cases2018")]


```

```{r}

# looking @ complete cases and missing cases (recommended by Dr. Labovitz)
lyme_c <- lyme_r[complete.cases(lyme_r), ]
dim(lyme_c)    

lyme_incomplete <- lyme_r[!complete.cases(lyme_r), ]
lyme_incomplete  #note to me: there are systemic incomplete cases in Connecticut

# looking @ distribution of lyme prevalence 
describe(lyme_r$Cases2018)     
hist(lyme_r$Cases2018, 
     breaks = 50,
     main = "Raw Lyme Cases 2018 (all counties)",
     xlab = "Case count")

```

```{r}
# how many counties had zero cases in 2018?
sum(lyme_r$Cases2018 == 0, na.rm = TRUE) 
sum(lyme_r$Cases2018 > 0, na.rm = TRUE) 

# do ststatus breakdown against zero/nonzero cases
table(lyme_r$ststatus, lyme_r$Cases2018 > 0)
```

```{r, population data loading and cleaning }

pop_raw     <- read.csv(here("data", "external", "population", 
                              "co-est2018-alldata.csv"))
# inspecting POPESTIMATE2018 is present
dim(pop_raw)     


# filtering to county-level rows only (dropping state totals)
pop_r <- pop_raw[pop_raw$SUMLEV == 50,
                 c("STATE", "COUNTY", "STNAME", "CTYNAME", "POPESTIMATE2018")]

dim(pop_r)    

# checking FIPS components
range(pop_r$STATE)
range(pop_r$COUNTY)

# missing values
pop_c <- pop_r[complete.cases(pop_r), ]
dim(pop_c)

# distribution check
describe(pop_r$POPESTIMATE2018)
hist(log(pop_r$POPESTIMATE2018),
     main = "Log County Population (2018 estimate)",
     xlab = "log(population)")
```

```{r, SAHIE uninsured}

sahie_raw   <- read.csv(here("data", "external", "insured", 
                              "sahie_2018.csv"), skip = 79)

dim(sahie_raw)   # has multiple rows per county?
names(sahie_raw) # checking PCTUI, statefips, countyfips, agecat, racecat, sexcat, iprcat are present

# checking how many unique counties before filtering
length(unique(paste0(sahie_raw$statefips, sahie_raw$countyfips)))

# inspecting the category filter values
table(sahie_raw$agecat)
table(sahie_raw$sexcat)
table(sahie_raw$iprcat)

sahie_r <- sahie_raw[sahie_raw$agecat == 0 & 
                     sahie_raw$racecat == 0 & 
                     sahie_raw$sexcat == 0 & 
                     sahie_raw$iprcat == 0 &
                     sahie_raw$geocat == 50,
                     c("statefips", "countyfips", "PCTUI", "pctui_moe", "county_name", "state_name")]

sahie_r$PCTUI <- as.numeric(sahie_r$PCTUI)

# checking how many NA in conversion
sum(is.na(sahie_r$PCTUI))

# dropping state rows
sahie_r <- sahie_r[sahie_r$countyfips != 0, ]

# checking what version values exist in the duplicated counties
table(sahie_r$version[duplicated(paste0(sahie_r$statefips, 
                                         sahie_r$countyfips)) |
                      duplicated(paste0(sahie_r$statefips, 
                                         sahie_r$countyfips), 
                                 fromLast = TRUE)])

# removing any remaining duplicate counties
sahie_r <- sahie_r[!duplicated(paste0(sahie_r$statefips, 
                                       sahie_r$countyfips)), ]

dim(sahie_r)
nrow(sahie_r) == length(unique(paste0(sahie_r$statefips, 
                                       sahie_r$countyfips)))  # should now be true?

dim(sahie_r)   # checking one row per county and same number of counties

nrow(sahie_r) == length(unique(paste0(sahie_r$statefips, sahie_r$countyfips)))  

#fixing duplicate counties ugh
dupes <- sahie_r[duplicated(paste0(sahie_r$statefips, sahie_r$countyfips)) | 
                 duplicated(paste0(sahie_r$statefips, sahie_r$countyfips), 
                            fromLast = TRUE), ]
dupes

# looking for missing values
sahie_c <- sahie_r[complete.cases(sahie_r), ]
dim(sahie_c)

sahie_incomplete <- sahie_r[!complete.cases(sahie_r), ]
sahie_incomplete

# looking @ distribution of prevalence
hist(sahie_r$PCTUI,
     breaks = 40,
     main = "Percent Uninsured by County (2018)",
     xlab = "% Uninsured (PCTUI)")
```

```{r, peromyscus leucopus occurrence records}

mouse_raw   <- read.delim(here("data", "external", "mice", 
                                "Mouse_0044277-260226173443078.csv"), 
                           sep = "\t", header = TRUE)

dim(mouse_raw)    # expect ~48,781 rows
names(mouse_raw)  # many columns — you only need a few

```
```{r}

# reducing to columns of interest
mouse_r <- mouse_raw[, c("gbifID", "stateProvince", "decimalLatitude", 
                          "decimalLongitude", "year", "occurrenceStatus",
                          "coordinateUncertaintyInMeters")]

# checking geographic scope within US
table(mouse_r$stateProvince) |> sort(decreasing = TRUE) |> head(20)

# checking coordinate quality
sum(is.na(mouse_r$decimalLatitude))    # records with no latidude
sum(is.na(mouse_r$decimalLongitude))   # records with no longitude

# flagging implausible coordinates (outside contiguous US bounding box)
bad_coords <- mouse_r[!is.na(mouse_r$decimalLatitude) & 
                       (mouse_r$decimalLatitude < 24 | mouse_r$decimalLatitude > 50 |
                        mouse_r$decimalLongitude < -125 | mouse_r$decimalLongitude > -66), ]
dim(bad_coords)   # how many records have weird coordinates?

# checking year range
describe(mouse_r$year)
hist(mouse_r$year,
     breaks = 50,
     main = "Peromyscus leucopus Records by Year",
     xlab = "Year of record")

# do records have no year at all
sum(is.na(mouse_r$year))

# missing values overall?
mouse_c <- mouse_r[complete.cases(mouse_r), ]
dim(mouse_c)

mouse_incomplete <- mouse_r[!complete.cases(mouse_r), ]
sapply(mouse_r, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))   # NA count per column
```

```{r, FIPS join}
#lyme set
lyme_r <- read_csv(here("data", "external","lyme_prevalence", "LD_Case_Counts_by_County_2023_updated.csv"))
# cleaned
lyme_clean <- lyme_r %>%
  mutate(fips = stcode * 1000 + ctycode) %>%  
  select(fips, stname, Ctyname, cases_2018 = Cases2018, ststatus)

unique(lyme_clean$ststatus)

# population cleaned set
pop_clean <- pop_r %>%
  rename_all(tolower) %>%
  mutate(fips = as.integer(state) * 1000 + as.integer(county)) %>%
  select(fips, pop_2018 = popestimate2018)

# SAHIE set
sahie_r <- read_csv(
  here("data", "external", "insured", "sahie_2018.csv"),
  skip = 79,
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

# verifying the header landed correctly before filtering
names(sahie_r)[1:6] 

#troubleshooting issues with header
raw_lines <- readLines(here("data", "external", "insured", "sahie_2018.csv"), n = 90)
grep("^year,", raw_lines) 

names(sahie_r)         
nrow(sahie_r)
head(sahie_r, 3)

```

```{r}

sahie_clean <- sahie_r %>%
  rename_all(tolower) %>%
  filter(
    year      == "2018",
    agecat    == "0",    # all ages
    racecat   == "0",    # all races
    sexcat    == "0",    # both sexes
    iprcat    == "0"     # all income levels
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    fips    = as.integer(statefips) * 1000 + as.integer(countyfips),
    pct_uninsured = as.numeric(pctui)
  ) %>%
# dropping state-level summary rows
  filter(countyfips != "000") %>%             
  select(fips, pct_uninsured)

# joining lyme, population, and sahie
analysis_df <- lyme_clean %>%
  left_join(pop_clean,   by = "fips") %>%
  left_join(sahie_clean, by = "fips")

# how many counties lost population after join? (hopefully none)
analysis_df %>%
  summarise(
    n_total        = n(),
    n_missing_pop  = sum(is.na(pop_2018)),
    n_missing_pctui = sum(is.na(pct_uninsured))
  )

#restricting counties to high incidence counties only
analysis_df <- analysis_df %>%
  filter(grepl("High Incid", ststatus)) %>%
  mutate(
    incidence_2018 = (cases_2018 / pop_2018) * 100000
  )

# inspecting  post join
nrow(analysis_df)       
sum(is.na(analysis_df$incidence_2018))   
summary(analysis_df$incidence_2018)     

hist(analysis_df$incidence_2018,
     main = "Lyme Incidence Rate 2018 (endemic counties)",
     xlab = "Cases per 100,000")

# saving/exporting new set
write_csv(analysis_df, here("data", "interim", "lyme_incidence_joined.csv"))

```

```{r}
#building weather variable based on multiple sets of NOAA data, one per month, for 2018
state_fips_lookup <- c(
  AL="01", AK="02", AZ="04", AR="05", CA="06", CO="08", CT="09",
  DE="10", DC="11", FL="12", GA="13", HI="15", ID="16", IL="17",
  IN="18", IA="19", KS="20", KY="21", LA="22", ME="23", MD="24",
  MA="25", MI="26", MN="27", MS="28", MO="29", MT="30", NE="31",
  NV="32", NH="33", NJ="34", NM="35", NY="36", NC="37", ND="38",
  OH="39", OK="40", OR="41", PA="42", RI="44", SC="45", SD="46",
  TN="47", TX="48", UT="49", VT="50", VA="51", WA="53", WV="54",
  WI="55", WY="56"
)
# reading all 12 monthly NOAA weather files
months <- c("jan", "feb", "mar", "apr", "may", "jun",
            "jul", "aug", "sep", "oct", "nov", "dec")

read_weather_month <- function(month_label) {
  
  filepath <- here("data", "external", "weather",
                   paste0("county_weather_", month_label, ".csv"))
  
  # skip to header
  df <- read_csv(filepath, skip = 3, show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  # renaming by position to avoid depending on exact header text
  df <- df |>
    rename(
      county_id   = 1,   
      county_name = 2,
      state_name  = 3,
      avg_temp_f  = 4    
    ) |>
    mutate(
      state_abbr  = str_extract(county_id, "^[A-Z]+"),
      county3     = str_pad(str_extract(county_id, "[0-9]+$"), 3, pad = "0"),
      state_fips2 = state_fips_lookup[state_abbr],
      fips        = as.integer(paste0(state_fips2, county3)),
      month       = month_label
    ) |>
    select(fips, month, avg_temp_f)
  
  return(df)
}

weather_long <- bind_rows(lapply(months, read_weather_month))

cat("Weather rows (long format):", nrow(weather_long), "\n")
cat("Unique counties:           ", n_distinct(weather_long$fips), "\n")
cat("Missing temperature values:", sum(is.na(weather_long$avg_temp_f)), "\n")
```


```{r}
# computing active-season months

# active_months_10c: months where avg temp >= 10C (50F) lower bound for I. scapularis questing. The cited upper bound of 60C is never practically reached in U.S. weather (whoops), so >= 10C is the filter my source is Fieser et al. (2021)

weather_county <- weather_long |>
  mutate(avg_temp_c = (avg_temp_f - 32) * (5 / 9)) |>
  group_by(fips) |>
  summarise(
    active_months_10c  = sum(avg_temp_c >= 10, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_annual_temp_c = round(mean(avg_temp_c, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n Weather variable summary (county level) \n")
print(summary(weather_county[, c("active_months_10c", "mean_annual_temp_c")]))

cat("\nLowest active months (expect northern / high-elevation counties):\n")
print(weather_county |> arrange(active_months_10c) |> head(6))

cat("\nHighest active months (expect Gulf Coast counties):\n")
print(weather_county |> arrange(desc(active_months_10c)) |> head(6))

# joining to interim Lyme dataset
lyme_interim <- read_csv(
  here("data", "interim", "lyme_incidence_joined.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

cat("\nLyme interim rows before join:", nrow(lyme_interim), "\n")

lyme_full <- lyme_interim |>
  left_join(weather_county, by = "fips")

cat("Rows after join: ", nrow(lyme_full), "\n")

no_weather <- lyme_full |> filter(is.na(active_months_10c))
cat("Counties with no weather match:", nrow(no_weather), "\n")
if (nrow(no_weather) > 0) {
  print(no_weather |> select(fips, stname, Ctyname, cases_2018))
}

# Connecticut FIPS (09110-09190) are a 2022 reorganization artifact; cases_2018 = NA were dropped and one county in DC (11001) has no NOAA county record was deleted. I need to make a note in my final report of all these missing values
```


```{r, final checks and saving}
# collinearity check

cat("\n Correlations among continuous predictors \n")
cor_check <- lyme_full |>
  select(incidence_2018, active_months_10c, mean_annual_temp_c, pct_uninsured) |>
  filter(complete.cases(pick(everything())))

print(round(cor(cor_check), 3))

# writing to processed folder 
write_csv(lyme_full, here("data", "processed", "lyme_analysis_full.csv"))

cat("\nWrote data/processed/lyme_analysis_full.csv\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(lyme_full), "| Columns:", ncol(lyme_full), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(names(lyme_full), collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

```{r, transforming and joining canopy data}

counties_sf <- tigris::counties(cb = TRUE, year = 2018, class = "sf") |>
   # removing alaska, hawaii, and territories
  filter(!STATEFP %in% c("02", "15", "60", "66", "69", "72", "78"))  

counties_sf <- st_transform(counties_sf, 5070)

counties_sf$fips          <- as.integer(paste0(counties_sf$STATEFP, 
                                                counties_sf$COUNTYFP))
counties_sf$county_area_m2 <- as.numeric(st_area(counties_sf))

dim(counties_sf)         
range(counties_sf$fips)  
```


```{r}
# downloading WBD HUC-12 boundaries directly from USGS regional files
options(timeout = 600)

wbd_dir <- here("data", "external", "natural_land", "WBD")
dir.create(wbd_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

huc2_regions <- sprintf("%02d", 1:18)

huc12_list <- lapply(huc2_regions, function(h2) {
  gpkg_path <- file.path(wbd_dir, paste0("WBD_", h2, ".gpkg"))
  
  # only download if not already in my folder
  if (!file.exists(gpkg_path)) {
    message("Downloading WBD region ", h2, " of 18...")
    url <- paste0(
      "https://prd-tnm.s3.amazonaws.com/StagedProducts/Hydrography/",
      "WBD/HU2/GPKG/WBD_", h2, "_HU2_GPKG.zip"
    )
    zip_path <- file.path(wbd_dir, paste0("WBD_", h2, ".zip"))
    download.file(url, zip_path, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
    
    # extracting just the .gpkg file — skip everything else in the zip
    zip_contents <- unzip(zip_path, list = TRUE)
    gpkg_name    <- zip_contents$Name[grepl("\\.gpkg$", zip_contents$Name)][1]
    unzip(zip_path, files = gpkg_name, exdir = wbd_dir)
    file.rename(file.path(wbd_dir, gpkg_name), gpkg_path)
    file.remove(zip_path)
    message("  Region ", h2, " saved.")
  } else {
    message("Region ", h2, " already cached, skipping download.")
  }
  
  sf::st_read(gpkg_path, layer = "WBDHU12", quiet = TRUE)
})

huc12_sf <- dplyr::bind_rows(huc12_list) |>
  st_transform(5070) |>
  rename(HUC_12 = huc12)

message("Total HUC-12 polygons loaded: ", nrow(huc12_sf))
```

```{r}
# setting active geometry column for huc12_sf 
st_geometry(huc12_sf) <- "shape"

huc12_geo  <- huc12_sf[, c("HUC_12")]
county_geo <- counties_sf[, c("fips", "county_area_m2")]

intersection <- st_intersection(huc12_geo, county_geo)
intersection$intersect_area_m2 <- as.numeric(st_area(intersection))
```

```{r}
# computing area weights and aggregate to county
crosswalk <- st_drop_geometry(intersection) |>
  mutate(weight = intersect_area_m2 / county_area_m2)

# loading PCanopy and join
pcanopy_raw <- read.csv(here("data", "external", "natural_land",
                              "CONUS_metrics_CSV", "PCanopy_CONUS.csv"))
# str_pad zero-pads to 12 characters from the left
pcanopy_raw$HUC_12 <- stringr::str_pad(
  sprintf("%.0f", as.numeric(pcanopy_raw$HUC_12)),
  width = 12, side = "left", pad = "0"
)

# verificaion
head(pcanopy_raw$HUC_12)
sum(crosswalk$HUC_12 %in% pcanopy_raw$HUC_12) 

crosswalk_lc <- crosswalk |>
  left_join(pcanopy_raw[, c("HUC_12", "pCanopy")], by = "HUC_12")

# aggregating to county
canopy_county <- crosswalk_lc |>
  group_by(fips) |>
  summarise(
    canopy_pct  = sum(pCanopy * weight, na.rm = TRUE),
    coverage_wt = sum(weight[!is.na(pCanopy)]),
    n_hucs      = n()
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# summary inspection
summary(canopy_county$canopy_pct)
sum(canopy_county$coverage_wt < 0.90)   # counties with poor coverage
```


```{r}
# joining to existing lyme data
lyme_joined <- read.csv(here("data", "processed", "lyme_analysis_full.csv"))

lyme_full <- lyme_joined |>
  left_join(canopy_county |> select(fips, canopy_pct, coverage_wt), 
            by = "fips")

message("Rows: ", nrow(lyme_full))
message("Missing canopy: ", sum(is.na(lyme_full$canopy_pct)))

#validating myself
cor(lyme_full$canopy_pct, lyme_full$incidence_2018, use = "complete.obs")

# saving
write.csv(lyme_full,
          here("data", "processed", "lyme_canopy_joined.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

message("Saved: data/processed/lyme_canopy_joined.csv")
message("Columns: ", paste(names(lyme_full), collapse = ", "))
```

```{r, mouse spatial join to county}
mouse_sf <- mouse_r |>
  filter(
    !is.na(decimalLatitude),
    !is.na(decimalLongitude),
    decimalLatitude  >= 24, decimalLatitude  <= 50,
    decimalLongitude >= -125, decimalLongitude <= -66
  ) |>
  st_as_sf(coords = c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude"), 
           crs = 4326) |>
  st_transform(5070)

# assigning each mouse record to a county
mouse_county_join <- st_join(mouse_sf, counties_sf[, c("fips", "county_area_m2")])

# counting occurrences per county, then computing density
mouse_density <- mouse_county_join |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  filter(!is.na(fips)) |>
  group_by(fips) |>
  summarise(
    mouse_records  = n(),
    county_area_m2 = first(county_area_m2)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    mouse_density_km2 = mouse_records / (county_area_m2 / 1e6)
  ) |>
  select(fips, mouse_records, mouse_density_km2)

# I am assigning counties with zero mouse records get density = 0
mouse_density_full <- counties_sf |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  select(fips) |>
  left_join(mouse_density, by = "fips") |>
  mutate(
    mouse_records     = replace_na(mouse_records, 0),
    mouse_density_km2 = replace_na(mouse_density_km2, 0)
  )

summary(mouse_density_full$mouse_density_km2)
```


```{r, mouse spatial join 2}
# joining to lyme canopy dataset
lyme_joined <- read.csv(here("data", "processed", "lyme_canopy_joined.csv"))

lyme_full2 <- lyme_joined |>
  left_join(mouse_density_full |> select(fips, mouse_density_km2), 
            by = "fips")

message("Missing mouse density: ", sum(is.na(lyme_full2$mouse_density_km2)))
cor(lyme_full2$mouse_density_km2, lyme_full2$incidence_2018, 
    use = "complete.obs")

write.csv(lyme_full2,
          here("data", "processed", "lyme_modeling_ready.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

message("Final dataset saved: data/processed/lyme_modeling_ready.csv")
message("Columns: ", paste(names(lyme_full2), collapse = ", "))
```
